title: Structural Ambiguity in BPE Tokenization: From Vocabulary Merges to Attention Collapse
description: JSON's structural grammar creates hidden boundaries in BPE tokenizer vocabularies. The opening quote character merges with common field names into single tokens on 4-5 of 8 tested tokenizers. We present a systematic cross-tokenizer analysis across 8 tokenizers from 6 providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Meta, Alibaba, DeepSeek, Google, Mistral). Fifteen common JSON field names (id, name, type, value, title, time, text, url, path, description, user_id, in, is, encoding, dns) exist as merged vocabulary entries on 50-63% of tokenizers. GPT-4 contains 114 quote+letter vocabulary entries; Claude and Gemma contain zero. On real evaluation data, JSON exhibits a boundary merge rate of 8.93% vs 1.00% for pipe-delimited formats (88.8% fewer merges). JSON overhead reaches 81% at 500 rows, growing O(n) per row. A grammar swap experiment (800 measurements) confirms token savings are structural, not delimiter-specific (0.4pp spread). These findings are irrecoverable: tokenizer vocabularies are frozen post-training, all model weights depend on them, and tokenization occurs before the transformer processes input. Correlation evidence from 2,400+ LLM evaluations shows JSON comprehension drops to 54.6% at 500-record scale.
notes: All experiments are reproducible from open-source scripts in the GCF repository (github.com/blackwell-systems/gcf/eval/). Comprehension evaluation data from 2,400+ LLM calls across 11 models.
